utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: The Trump administration's singular focus on thwarting renewable energy has been tragically self-defeating for our country, and it continues to set us back in our fight to save our futures every day.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: That said, I commend EDC for continuing to focus on what we can do here in New York City with projects like Batworks, Blue Highway Action Plan, and advancing the plan for the Brooklyn Marine Terminal.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: In addition, the advisory council focused on an issue that is a core part of Solar One's mission, readying the green workforce of tomorrow with the skills that they need today.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: Along with our partner organizations who have been represented here today, like NYC ETC, Solar One has made great strides in the field of green workforce development.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: At our Long Island City,

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: In Workforce Training Center, we have four classrooms where we are teaching adults who are facing barriers to employment in green building operations and maintenance, green construction, plumbing, electrical, solar installation, building decarbonization, HVAC installation, and maintenance.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: Each year, we graduate around 800 individuals from our program, and each of those individuals carries with them approximately three professional certifications.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: There are many initiatives underway that you've heard about, but I would like to, if I was to choose one to see the city focus on, it would be engaging with the universe of building management firms to engage in a robust and sustained way with the

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: green workforce development providers to both ensure that the skills that we are teaching in an ever-evolving environment are meeting the needs of employers.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: Including with building automation technologies, and that there's an ongoing partnership with green workforce providers and unions like 32BJ and Local 3 to ensure that there's a pipeline of candidates for the tens of thousands of jobs in New York City in the green economy over the next decades.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Stephen Levin
utterances.text: Thank you very much.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you for your testimony.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: And lastly, we have one testimony over Zoom, Vinny Dong.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Angela Nuri-San
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Vinnie Thong, a 15-year-old student at Queens High School for the Sciences, testifies about the importance of youth internships and green jobs in NYC public schools. He urges sustaining Future Ready programs and expanding sustainability initiatives across all schools, noting that today's students will be in their 30s when the GEEP's full effects are realized.
chapters.title: Vinnie Thong (student) on youth engagement in Green Economy Action Plan

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: Good afternoon, Committee on Economic Development and Workforce Development.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: My name is Vinnie Thong, and I'm a current student at Queens High School for the Sciences.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: I am here to testify about the Green Economy Action Plan.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: As a student, it is essential that the EDC and the City Council focuses on youth internships and jobs.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: Specifically, it is essential that New York City public schools are considered sustainability initiatives.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: Implementing sustainability projects within the department.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: It is also essential that New York City public schools sustain future-ready programs and help grow this program to all schools across public schools.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: As a student, we are the future of

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: New York City and it is essential that student voice and student engagement is heard throughout this process.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: Currently, I am 15 and in about 20 years when I see the full effect of the Green Economy Action Plan, I will be around my 30s.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: It is essential that the youth of today are considered in

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: The changes we make and the decisions we make today

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: To ensure that the changes we make today is sustainable.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: That would be all.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Vinnie Thong
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council staff notes two additional Zoom registrants who did not testify, and concludes the hearing.
chapters.title: Staff concludes hearing

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you very much.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: And there are two folks who registered via Zoom to testify.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Andy Isenson.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Okay, and Ramona Fureya.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: All right, that concludes our hearing.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you very much.

summary: Joint hearing of the Committees on Economic Development and Workforce Development examining implementation of the Green Economy Action Plan (GEEP), which projects 400,000 green jobs by 2040 but has seen only ~2,000 net new green jobs since 2023.

Council members pressed EDC and NYC Talent on job creation tracking, the $600 million youth workforce funding breakdown, training facility timelines (only 1 of 5 borough facilities open), and whether the plan remains viable amid federal funding rollbacks and a new administration.

Public testimony from 12 workforce development organizations emphasized stronger employer-provider partnerships, better data tracking and dashboards, sustained investment in green career pathways for barriered New Yorkers, and the urgent need for a permanent EDC president.
title: Committee on Economic Development
